hook: Who among the 105 Kuru princes promised Drona that he would take revenge on King Drupada on his behalf?
explanation: After Bhishma appointed Drona as the Guru of the Kuru princes, Drona asked the princes to fulfil his wish of revenge against Drupada, once their studies were over. Only Arjuna came forward to take this oath. Drona smelled Arjuna’s head and from that day he became the Guru’s favorite disciple. This gain shows Drona’s complex personality – he asked for Guru Dakshina in advance and that too a specific wish. Normally, Dakshina is voluntary made after the studies are over. He promised to make Arjuna the greatest archer in the world in return of his promise to take revenge on his behalf.

hook: When did Arjuna realise that he could shoot in the dark guided only by sound?
explanation: Once Arjuna was eating by candle light. Due to strong winds, the candle blew off. But Arjuna continued eating without a hitch as if by reflex. He then realized that if he could eat so easily in the dark, he can as well as shoot arrows in the dark. He then started practicing during the night and developed the skill of using the shabd-bedhi baan (noise penetrating arrow). He could estimate where the target was based on the noise coming from around the target and shoot at the target accurately. A good student can develop many new skills if he/she is rightly mentored and is willing to put in hard work and rigorous practice.

hook: What did Arjuna see when he was asked to aim at an artificial bird hung on a tree?
explanation: Arjuna was very focused as a student. So when he was asked to aim at a target, he only saw the target. When Duryodhana was asked to aim at the target, he said that he saw the beautiful scenery around the target. Maybe as a son of a blind father, he was more interested in seeing beauty than the target. Bheema, in contrast, wanted to bring down the full tree instead of aiming at the target. Perhaps it was his egoistic nature speaking.

hook: When Drona went to the river, his leg was caught by an animal. All other princes were scared but Arjuna shot an arrow to kill the animal. Which animal was it?
explanation: Probably it was a test designed by Drona. He created a situation wherein it appeared that his leg had been caught by a crocodile underwater. All other princes froze with fear but Arjuna was undeterred. He accurately shot at the animal even though underwater the shape of the animal seen is distorted by refraction. As per physics, light gets bent while passing from air to water, a phenomenon called refraction.

hook: Drona was partial to Ashwathama. Who was Ashwathama?
explanation: Ashwathama was the son of Drona & Kripi. As guru, Drona was very partial to son. He made an announcement that whichever student fulfilled a certain task would get special lessons. The task was to go to the river and fill a pot with water and bring it back. All other princes got pots with narrow openings. Only Ashwathama got a pot with wide mouth. This made sure that he would fill up the pot faster and be automatically be eligible for the special lessons. This was another instance of Drona not being completely scrupulous in his role of Guru.

hook: Drona refused to teach Eklavya who was a prince (kumara) of which community?
explanation: Eklavya is described as a Nishaada Kumara. The Nishaadas lived in forests. Drona refused to teach Eklavya when he approached him. Perhaps he wanted to teach only city based disciples. Whatever be the reason, Drona had many failings as a guru. Eklavya, the dedicated student was undeterred. He built a statue of Drona, accepted it as his guru and started practicing. He ended up developing great skills as an archer.

hook: What was the relation between Hiranya-dhanusha and Eklavya?
explanation: Eklavya was the son of Hiranya-dhanusha who was not only the leader of Nishaadas but was also an important general of Jarasandha, king of Magadha. Jarasandha was a mortal enemy of Krishna.

hook: Eklavya shot arrows into the mouth of which animal to stop it from making noise?
explanation: The Kuru princes go to a forest to hunt, accompanied by dogs. One of the dogs wandered where Eklavya was practicing and started barking. To shut the dog up, Eklavya shot a bunch of arrows into the dog’s mouth but without hurting it. The princes were astounded by the skill of the archer and went back and narrated the story to Guru Drona. Arjuna complained to Guru Drona that he had failed his promise to make him the best as Eklavya was the better archer.

hook: What did Drona ask Eklavya to give as guru dakshina?
explanation: Drona was told of Eklavya’s great skill as an archer. Arjuna was upset that Dronacharya has not kept his promise to make Arjuna the greatest archer. Drona came to the forest to meet Eklavya. He found out that Eklavya used to practice next to his clay idol, which he treated as his guru. Drona was quick to latch on to this and ask for guru Dakshina. He asked for Eklavya’s right thumb which would cripple his future as an archer. This is a story of dubious ethical dimensions and is used to suggest that a lower status Nishaada archer was disadvantaged at the hands of a Kuru prince. The story is bit more complex than that. Eklavya was the prince of Nishaadas who had their own kingdom and not a low status person. His father was the general of Jarasandha, the king of Magadha. It was perhaps Drona’s anxiety to meet his promise to his employers that drove him to the macabre act. We don’t know. But this act again brings out the negative characteristics of Drona’s character.

hook: After the end of education of Kuru princes, a talent show was organized to showcase their skills. Who gatecrashed the event?
explanation: The Kuru princes were displaying their skills at the talent show. Arjuna was obviously the best. Suddenly, a dashing warrior wearing armour (kavach) and ear-rings (kundala) appeared and challenged Arjuna. He matched the feats of Arjuna and asked for one on one competition. He was asked whether he was a prince as the event was open to princes only. At this question, Karna hung his head. Adiratha the charioteer came in and it was clear that he was Karna’s father. Bheema started insulting Karna’s family background. It was then the event was called off. Later, everyone would know that Karna was Kunti’s son and the brother of the Pandavas. But that is another story.

hook: Duryodhana made Karna the king of which kingdom?
explanation: When Karna came to the talent show uninvited, Duryodhana immediately recognized that Karna could be a useful ally. When he came to know that Karna could not participate because he was not a prince, he immediately made Karna the king of Anga. This kingdom is on the border of present day Bihar & W Bengal. But we know that Duryodhana was not eligible to make the donation of this kingdom. We don’t know if Hastinapur was the overlord of Anga. In any case, Duryodhana was only a prince and could not declare anyone king. What we know is that it was not a good gift. A good gift (sattav-dana) is when it is made without expecting anything in return. A moderate gift, Rajas-dana is when a gift with some specific return in mind. A bad gift, Tamas-dana is a gift made to an undeserving person.

hook: Vyasa means reorganizer. What did Vyasa reorganize?
explanation: Vyasa means reorganizing. Ved Vyasa reorganized and compiled the Vedas into four parts. He taught one Veda each to four of his students and tasked them with the propagation. His name is Krishna Dvaipayana. Dvaipayana refers to him having been born on an island. Dvaipa means an island. Vyasa also means diameter of a circle and the longest line that can be drawn across the circle. This is also interpreted to mean that Vyasa covers the entire earth in Mahabharata.

hook: Vyasa was a prolific composer also. Which of these works is NOT authored by him?
explanation: Vyasa composed a massive corpus. He composed the mahapuranas. He also composed the Brahmasutras which are summaries of the Vedanta and guide to life. Upanishads are a part of the Vedic corpus and were not composed by Ved Vyasa..

hook: Mahabharata is the longest epic in the world. How many verses does Mahabharata have?
explanation: Mahabharata has one lakh (i.e a hundred thousand) verses. The critical edition of Mahabharata removed several verses that were not considered part of the original text. It still is a mammoth 80,000 verses. There are four other epics in the world, including Ramayana. Mahabharata is longer than all other epics put together.

hook: What does Maha mean in Mahabharata?
explanation: Maha means large. It truly captures the mammoth nature of the work. It is not just long in length, but also in its coverage. It describes a large geography from east to west. It also covers the entire lineage of Bharata kings, stretching across 48 generations. There are also hundreds of sub stories within Mahabharata. It even includes a summary of Ramayana within it.

hook: Which is called Panchamaveda?
explanation: Mahabharat contains the essence of all Vedas in story form. Therefore, it is also referred to as Panchama Veda, or the fifth Veda.

hook: Ma Dhvayam Bha Dhvayam… What does this sloka mean?
explanation: There are 18 Mahapuranas. This verse helps us remember the names of the Mahapuranas. It means 2 Pruanas starting with Ma - Matsya and Markandeya. 2 Puranas starting with Bha - Bhavishya and Bhagavata 3 Puranas starting with Bra - Brahma, Brahmanda and Brahma Vaivarta 4 Puranas starting with Va - Vayu, Vamana, Vishnu and Varaha 7 purana each starting with other letters - Agni, Narada, padma, Linha, Garuda, Kurma and Skanda.

hook: How many Mahapuranas are there
explanation: There are 18 Mahapuranas. In addition there are 18 upapuranas which are lesser known. Several other compilations are also referred to as Puranas, outside of the Mahapurana and Upapurana categories.

hook: Which of these is a name for Mahabharata?
explanation: Jaya is one of the older names for Mahabharata. Jaya means victory.

hook: Jaya means victory. Whose victory does the term describe?
explanation: Jaya refers to the victory of Dharma. Whether Pandavas win or Kauravas win is irrelevant. Whichever side is with Dharma that wins.Yato Dharmastato Jayaḥ is a phrase that is in Mahabharata and it means, Where there is Dharma, there will be Victory. This phrase is so powerful that it has been adopted as the motto of the Supreme Court of India.

hook: Naimisaranya is where Mahabharata is narrated. Where is Naimisaranya?
explanation: Naimisaranya is near Lucknow in Uttar Pradesh. It is on the banks of the Gomati river. Naimisaranya is the place where Mahabharata is narrated by Ugrashrava Sauti. Naimisaranya is one of the most sacred tirthas of India. It was abode of tens of thousands of Rishis. The Ramayana, the Mahabharata and puranas are all believed to have been recited here.

hook: Ugrashrava Sauti narrates Mahabharata. What does the name Suta mean?
explanation: Story telling was serious profession in olden days. The profession of story telling was Important in the propagation of itihaas. This practice also maintained the authenticity of the narration across generations. The earliest story tellers were Rama’s sons Luv and Kusha. In fact, Rama hears the story of Ramayana from his sons, though he does not know that they are his sons. This tradition of professional story telling still continues now in the form of Hari Katha.

hook: What does Critical Edition do?
explanation: Critical Edition refers to a version of ancient texts. A critical edition attempts to recreate the original version of the text, discarding insertions that may have been made over a period of time and in different languages. It involves three very important and tedious steps. It collects all versions of the text from various sources and languages. A group of experts make judgements on which verser are authentic and which are later day interpolations. Finally, the authentic version is published, with all the other versions of each verse and comments included separately. The Bhandarkar Oriental Research Institute compiled the Critical Edition of Mahabharata. It was a giant research project with research conducted over five decades consulting 1,259 manuscripts. The Critical Edition of the Mahabharata has 89000+ verses and is published in 19 Volumes with over 15000 large size pages.

hook: Which books are NOT recommended reading?
explanation: It is important to read books where the writing and spirit of the original author are reproduced faithfully. When an author injects his own interpretations and value systems, then the reader is being given a different version from what the original author intended.

hook: Which narration are we reading when we read Mahabharata
explanation: We did not hear Mahabharata directly from Ved Vyasa. Ved Vyasa narrated Mahabharata to his student Vaisampayana first. Vaisampayana then narrates the Mahabharat to King Janamejaya. Ugrashrava Sauti who was present at the occasion hears the narration and then travels to Naimisharanya. Here he meets Rishi Saunaka and countless other Rishis. He narrates Mahabharat to the gathering of Rishis. This narration is what we read. Thus, it would be the third narration, the first being to Vaisampayana, the second being to Janamejaya and the third being to the Rishis.

hook: Which of these is not the name of Draupadi?
explanation: Drupada takes a vow that he will have two children, a Son who will defeat Drona and a daughter who will marry Arjuna. He performed a Yaga and two children were born, Drishtadyumna and daughter Draupadi. Draupadi’s other names are Krishnaa, Yaajnaseni (because she was the daughter of Draupada who was also called Yaajnasena) and Paanchaali (because she is from the kingdom of Paanchaala).

hook: Who did the people of Hastinapura want as their King?
explanation: Hastinapura people started saying they want Pandavas, especially Yudishtira to be their Kings. Duryodhana started becoming jealous since his father had lost out on being a King because he was blind. Now he and his brothers are also losing out.

hook: Who was Duryodhana most jealous of?
explanation: Duryodhana had been jealous of Bheema since the young age. He wanted to kill Bheema. They tried several ways to kill Bheema, including drowning him in the river. They also tried poisoning him.

hook: Where did Duryodhana decide to send Pandavas to?
explanation: People realized Kauravas are jealous of Pandavas and that they cannot be good Kings. Duryodhana learns this through a spy. He decides that Pandavas have to be driven away. Duryodhana says let us send them away to Varanavata. A Yatra celebration of Pashupati (Shiva) was about to happen there. He decided to build a palace from wax in Varanavata where Pandavas would stay and to later on burn the palace.

hook: Yatra is held for what reason?
explanation: The deity is brought out so that people who cannot visit due to old age or handicap can have Darshana of the deity. The Puri Jagannath Yatra is one of the famous Yatras of India. Almost all temples in South India have a Rath Yatra. The story of Mahabharata is 5000 years old. Yatra Mahotsava used to happen even at that time. If you visit Udupi, you can see the Rathaveethi around the SriKrishna temple. You can about some famous Yatras through a quiz at https://indiyatra.in/rath-yatra-quiz/

hook: Who warned Yudhishtira about the plans to kill Pandavas in Varanavata?
explanation: Vidura is also the uncle of Pandavas, as well as Kauravas. He informs Yudhisthira in a coded language. Only Vidhura and Yudhisthira could understand this language. While in Varanavata, Kunti asks Yudhishtira what had Vidura told him. Bheema and Yudhishtira realise that the palace is made of wax.

hook: Who was killed in the palace fire instead of Pandavas?
explanation: With the help of diggers, the Pandavas created an undergound tunnel leading from the palace outside. One day a Huntress comes with five of their children. They wanted to stay with Pandavas. Purocana was supposed to burn the palace the same day. Bheema himself puts the palace on hire and escapes. The huntress and the children died in the palace and were mistaken for Pandavas and Kunti. Bheema carried the Pandavas and Kunti and escaped. There is a famous story about the secret passage in the Chitradurga fort which is in Karnataka. Find out more about it.

hook: How does Bheema meet Hidimbaa?
explanation: After Pandavas escape from the wax palace, all the Pandavas rest under a tree. They ask Bheema to fetch water. He rushes to get water. He does not have a container and tries to carry water in his upper garment. A Rakshasa named Hidimbaasura started smelling human flesh. He had a sister called Hidimbaa. He sent his sister to find out more details. Hidimbaa fell in love with Bheema and wanted to marry him. There is a temple for Hidimbaa in a famous tourist place. Search and find out where it is! Once you have found that out, search for another Hidimbaa temple that is there in the same state. Post a picture of the second temple and a short write-up in the reply to the quiz in the Course page. A lucky winner will be chosen from those who post. Prize is a copy of Essential Mahabharata book.

hook: What is the name of the child of Bheema and Hidimbaa?
explanation: Hidimbaa was in love with Bheema and wanted to marry him. Kunti agrees that Bheema can marry her. However, Hidimbaa mentions that Bheema has to fight his brother. Bheema defeats and kills Hidimbasura and then marries Hidimbaa. Bheema and Hidimbaa give birth to Ghatokacha. He immediately became a strong man and promised to come to the Pandavas help whenever asked. Eventually he would end up saving the life of Arjuna. You will find out in latter classes. There is an interesting very old and funny Tamil song called Kalyana Samaiyal Sadam which features Ghatotkacha. Post a youtube link of this song in the course page. One lucky winner will receive a copy of the Essential Mahabharata book. But the child has to sing two or three verses of this song on 16th April class at the end.

hook: Pandavas go to Ekachakranagara on the advice of…….
explanation: Bakaasura was constantly troubling everybody in Ekachakranagara. The King of Ekachakranagara escaped from the town and was staying in a safe place. This is not a behaviour that King should have. The King should actually protect his people rather than escape. A king should never be selfish. The Pandavas take shelter in Ekachakranagara in the house of a Brahmin.

hook: According to Vyasa, who is the trunk of the tree that represents Kauravas?
explanation: _x000D_ _x000D_ Vyasa compared Pandavas and Kauravas to a huge tree. He mentions that Duryodhana is a giant tree filled with jealousy. The root of the tree is Dritharashtra who is an ignoble person. He is not giving the right advice to his children. The trunk of the tree is Karna who is supporting all these immoral activities. The fruits and flowers of the tree are Dushasana.

hook: Who represents the fruits of the tree of Pandavas according to Vyasa?
explanation: _x000D_ _x000D_ The tree of the Pandavas is filled with Dharma. It is held by the trunk of Bhima and Arjuna and the root is Sri Krishna himself. The fruits of the tree are the children of Madri.

hook: What was the most difficult thing that each family by turn had to send to Bakassasura?
explanation: Bakaasura was constantly troubling the people of Ekacharanagara. He had come up with a rule that everyday one house should send a bullock-cart full of food alongwith one person from the family. Bakaasura would eat the food, the bulls and the person coming with the food. Everyday one person has to be sacrificed for the Asura. The Pandavas came to this village in disguise and stayed in the village in a Brahmana’s family. They would go around the village everyday begging for food. One day Kunti heard the host family crying. It was their turn to supply food. Each member of the family was volunteering to go. Kunti offered to send her child to Bakaasura.

hook: Who does Kunti send to Bakaasura?
explanation: Bhima goes with the bullock cart and food to the top of the hill where Bakaasura is staying. Bakssasura is craving for food, smelling human flesh and is becoming impatient. But Bheema starts eating the food in front of Bakaasura. This annoys Bakaasura even more. Bakaasura punches Bheema which actually helps Bheema digest the food. They start fighting and Bheema kills Bakaasura. He carries the dead body of Bakaasura and quietly leaves the body outside the village. This is because he did not want to reveal the identity.

hook: Draupadi’s Swayamwara was held in the city of a childhood friend of Drona. Which city is this?
explanation: The Pandavas come to the outskirts of the city called Panchalanagara. It was ruled by Yajnasena who was also called Drupada. He was a childhood friend of Vyasa when they were studying under the same Guru. Drupada had two children, Dhrishtadumna and Draupadi who was also called Panchaali. The King had arranged a Swayamwara for Panchaali. As a challenge a fish had to be shot by looking at its reflection in a bowl. There was also a rotating wheel below the fish which made the task more difficult. All the greatest princes of Hastinapura could not even lift the bow. Arjuna, in the disguise of a Brahmana, shoots the fist. Pandavas fought the other losing Kings and retuned victorious with Draupadi.

hook: What is Kunti to Krishna?
explanation: Kunti is the sister of Vasudeva who is the father of Krishna. Krishna had also come to Panchalanagara since Kunti was his aunt. He wanted to help her and Pandavas who were his cousins. Krishna and Balarama come to the hut where the Pandavas are staying. He bows down to Kunti, Yudhishtira and Bhima. Krishna hugs Arjuna. Nakula and Sahadeva do pranams to Krishna as they are younger. Krishna promises the Pandavas that he will be there to help them whenever needed.

hook: Kunti mistakenly asked Pandavas to share Draupadi. When Pandavas came home she mistakenly thought they have .....
explanation: When Pandavas returned home after winning Swayamwara, they announced that they had won the Bhiksha for the day. Mistakenly, Kunti, thinking they have brought food, carelessly said that all five Pandavas should share the Bhiksha. By then all Pandavas had fallen in love with Draupadi and Draupadi had also fallen in love with all the Pandavas. Therefore all the Pandavas ended up marring Draupadi. This also kept the already united brothers even more united.

hook: Draupadi in her earlier life had prayed for a good husband and by chance was blessed five times. Who blessed her?
explanation: In her previous life, Draupadi had done Tapasya for a husband. When Shiva had come to bless her, in excitement, she hurriedly said, I need a husband five times. Shiva had also said “so be it “ five times in response. This is probably the reason Draupadi obtained five husbands in Mahabharata.

hook: How does Drupada realise that the Brahmins who came to the Swayamwara were Kshatriyas?
explanation: Dhrishtadyumna spied on the Pandavas when they were discussing arms and kingdom. He realizes that these people are Kshatriyas and tells Drupada. A happy Drupada arranges a grand wedding for Draupadi. Now they have the support of a huge kingdom of Panchala through marriage and also Krishna.

hook: The Pandavas were in disguise but after marrying Draupadi, they go back to Hastinapura and reveal their identity? Why did they do it?
explanation: After they got married to Draupadi, Pandavas had the support of a powerful kingdom of Panchala. Moreover, Krishna had also visited them and offered support. Therefore Pandavas got the confidence that they can now take on Kauravas.

hook: Who was the ally of Jarasandha whom Krishna killed at the Rajasuya yagya for repeatedly abusing him?
explanation: Shishupla was one of the closest ally of Jarasandha. He was also related to Krishna. He was angry at the killing of Jarasandha and vehemently opposed Yudhistira when he anointed Krishna as the chief guest. Shishupala kept on insulting Krishna by abusing him and his family and refused to stop when asked by others. Krishna then killed him for disrupting the sacred occasion.

hook: Who was sent by the Kauravas to invite Yudhistira to a game of dice?
explanation: After being insulted at the Rajasuya yajna, Duryodhana comes back and devises a plan to hurt the Pandavas. He wants to have a gambling match or a dice game with them wherein he could snatch the Pandavas’ prosperity. Dhritrashtra agrees and sends Vidura to invite Yudhistira. Vidura wants a non-competitive match but the Kauravas don’t agree. They know that Yudhistira is addicted to gambling and will not refuse. Krishna had warned Yudhistira against such an invitation but to no avail. Yudhistira uses the excuse that it a kshatriya’s dharma not to refuse such an invitation. But this is a partial understanding. If such a move leads to destruction of one’s family & kingdom, it can’t be dharma.

hook: Whom did Duryodhana choose to play on his behalf during the game of dice?
explanation: Shakuni was the brother of the mother of Kauravas, Gandhari. They were from the royal family of Gandharas. Shakuni was an expert in gambling and he had long cuboidal dices which listened to his commands. That’s why he always won. Some regional Mahabharatas have a story of how Shakuni and his family had been imprisoned and starved by the Kurus. The game of dice that he played with Yudhistira was his way of humiliating the Kuru family. More likely, he was just an evil person. In the game with Yudhistira, he won all of the riches and property of the Pandavas. He then goaded Yudhistira to put his brothers and wife at stake and Shakuni won them. So addicted to gambling was Yudhistira that all his learning did not help in making a simple choice of not putting his family to shame.

hook: Who dragged Draupadi by her hair to the sabha and then tried to disrobe her?
explanation: Once Yudhistira had lost Draupadi at the dice game, she became a slave of the Kauravas. Duryodhana sent a courtier Pratikami to get her to the sabha from the women’s quarters but she refused to come. He then sent his loyal acolyte and younger brother, Dushashna to get her. He knew that he would execute his orders, come what may. When Draupadi refused to come to the sabha again, he dragged her by the hair and brought her. In the sabha, he shamefully tried to disrobe her, thereby getting infamy as the eternal villain.

hook: Who along with Duryodhana, Shakuni & Dushashna, was the fourth member of the ‘Dushta-chatushtaya’, four villains who tormented Draupadi in the sabha?
explanation: Karna had many good qualities. But in the episode in the sabha, he along with the others was extremely mean and disrespectful to Draupadi. He called her abusive names and even goaded Dushashna to shame her.

hook: Bhishma let down Draupadi when she appealed to him in the sabha to save her. What did he tell her?
explanation: Draupadi is a heroine who did not lose her head even under extreme stress. When she was dragged to the sabha, he first saluted the elders and then asked a subtle question to Bhishma. She asked whether Yudhistira had first lost her or himself at the dice game. Having lost himself, he may have lost the right to stake his wife. But Bhishma was unable to think clearly and said “Dharma is subtle,” to justify his inaction.

hook: Who was the only Kaurava present who opposed the treatment being meted to Draupadi in the sabha?
explanation: Vikarna was a junior Kaurava, a younger brother of Duryodhana. He was the only clear headed person in the sabha that day. He forcefully opposed what was being done to Draupadi. The Pandavas were always grateful to him for this. Vikarna chose to fight for the Kauravas in the great war as he was a loyal brother. When Bheema killed him, he recalled how he had tried to save Draupadi.

hook: Why is Draupadi called ‘nathavati anathavat’?
explanation: Draupadi had five husbands but none came to her rescue when she was being humiliated in the sabha. Nathavati – with husbands, anathavat – without protection.

hook: Who was the only Pandava who protested the humiliation that Draupadi was subjected to in the sabha?
explanation: When Draupadi was being subjected to humiliation, the Pandavas did not react as they could not intervene, having been reduced to the status of slaves by then. The irrepressible Bheema could not hold back. He threatened the Kauravas but he was checked by Arjuna who told him that they could not go against their elder brother Yudhistira. Later, Draupadi would always turn to Bheema for help before any of her other husbands. After they were freed, Bheema swore to break Duryodhana’s thigh as he had invited Draupadi to sit on his thighs. He also swore to tear off Dushashna’s arms which he had used to drag Draupadi.

hook: Who saved Draupadi from being disrobed in the sabha?
explanation: As Dushashna tugged at Draupadi’s sari, she held on to her sari with one hand and raised the other calling out to Krishna for help. In North Indian versions of the epic, it is said that Krishna responded only when she raised both her hands in total surrender to God, signifying importance of bhakti. She was then supplied with endless saris to cover her. In the original version, Vyasa says that she did not cry to Krishna and saris appeared on their own. Later interpreters suggested that it could only be the work of Krishna. Others might suggest that it was the audience sitting in the sabha that kept on throwing their clothes to her to save her from shame.

hook: Who rescued the Pandavas from slavery after Yudhistira lost their freedom to the Kauravas during the dice game?
explanation: Dushashna was unable to disrobe Draupadi even after trying shamelessly as endless saris kept on appearing to save her. Dhritrashtra got frightened at these turns of event. He freed Draupadi and granted her two wishes. With the first wish she freed Yudhistira from slavery. With the second wish, she freed her other husbands. Draupadi maintained her composure and chose not to be angry with her husbands at their mistakes that had caused her so much discomfort. Of course, later in the story she does berate her husbands for their behaviour. So impressed was Dhritrashtra with her conduct that he returned their kingdom to the Pandavas and sent them back.

hook: After Yudhistira lost the gambling rematch that he foolishly agreed to, the Pandavas underwent an exile of 12 years and ‘agyatvaas’ for a year. What did agyatvaas entail?
explanation: Duryodhana didn’t take kindly to the fact that his father had returned all that he had won from Yudhistira. Like always, he bullied his father and got him to send an invitation for a gambling rematch to Yudhistira. In an act of unexplainable foolishness, Yudhistira agreed. Shakuni once again played on Duryodhana’s behalf and easily defeated Yudhistira. This time, the Pandavas were asked to go into exile. A special feature was that they had to live disguised and incognito for a year following the 12 years of exile.

hook: Kunti did not accompany her sons in their exile after the gambling match. Whom did she stay with at Hastinapura?
explanation: During their earlier exile after the lakshagruh episode, it was Kunti who guided and accompanied the Panadavas. This time, that role was played by the wife of the Pandavas, Draupadi. Kunti found herself living among the hostile Kauravas at Hastinapur. She stayed with Vidura, who was not only the half brother of her husband but was the most inclined to dharma at Hastinapur.

hook: Where does Dritharastra send Pandavas after they return from Draupadi swayamvara?
explanation: When pandavas returned to Hastinapur after Draupadi swayamwara, Dhritarashtra was worried. He felt that Duryodhana would get jealous of his cousins and then create conflicts. To prevent this, Dhritarashtra sent Pandavas to Khandava-prastha.

hook: What is the name of the city built by Pandavas?Why was it named so?
explanation: When the Pandavas arrived at Khandava-prastha, they established a city which was very prosperous. The prosperity matched that of Indra's capital, hence Khandava-prastha was renamed as Indraprastha.

hook: The capital of Indira's kingdom was called?
explanation: Lord Indira lived in Devaloka and his kingdom was very prosperous. The capital of his kingdom was called Amaravathi which was prosperous and a beautiful city.

hook: Which of the following is not a use of a cow?
explanation: The cow is the most sacred animal for sanathanis. It is also known as Kamadhenu. Every product of cow is useful, for eg. milk from which we get curds, butter and ghee, Cow dung is a good fertilizer and fuel. The bull is used for transportation and in farming.

hook: What did Arjuna do as a repentance for entering Yudhisthira's chamber?
explanation: The Pandavas had reached an agreement that they would spend one year with Draupadi by turns and the others would not enter their house for that period of time. Once a Brahmin requested Arjuna's help to get back his cows. Arjuna was forced to enter Yudhisthira's house as all the weapons were kept there. By this act Arjuna had broken his promise and therefore he undertook a teertha yatra as repentance.

hook: What does TeerthaYatra do to a person?
explanation: Teertha is derived from ” taraṇāt tīrtha ityāhuḥ” which is a journey where one enhances their spirituality. Arjuna visited a number of places and took blessings. Many of those places like Kashi, Prayaga and others are teertha kshetras even today.

hook: Uloopi was the princess of which place?
explanation: Uloopi was a princess from the Nagavanhsa. Arjuna married her. According to the customs of that place the princess and the son had to stay in their parents’ place even after marriage. Hence she remained in Nagaloka even after her marriage to Arjuna.

hook: Who was the princess of Manipur and who was her son?
explanation: When Arjuna went on a teertha yatra he visited a number of places. He also married the princess of Manipur(Malalulupura). He had a son from her named Babruvahana.

hook: Who was Subhadra and Arjuna’s son?
explanation: Arjuna visited Dwarka during his teertha yatra and married Krishna's sister Subhadra. Their son was Abhimanyu.

hook: Which Yagna did Yudhistira perform and what was the aim?
explanation: Yudhistitra performed the Rajasuya Yagna which is one of the Dharma yagnas performed for the unification of the country. He invited various kings and discussed the need to unite. He convinced them of the need to remain united and thus accept him as a Dharmic King.

hook: What was the name of Jarasandha’s capital city?
explanation: Jarasandha was the father in law of Kamsa and his daughters Asthi and Prapti were married to him. Jarasandha was angry that Krishna had killed Kamsa and constantly attacked Krishna.

hook: What was the disguise worn by Arjuna, Bhima and Krishna when they went to kill Jarasandha?
explanation: They dressed as Snaatakas ( young graduates) to avoid identification. Krishna wanted to avoid a full fledged war, to avoid the death of innocent citizens. Bhima challenged Jarasandha for one to one combat and killed him.

hook: Choose the wrong match from the choice given below regarding the responsibilities given to people during Rajasuya Yagna.
explanation: When Yudhisthira performed the Rajasuya Yagna, the family members were assigned with different tasks. Duryodhana was in charge of collecting the tribute from different kings and not in-charge of security.

hook: Who suggested Krishna's name to receive Agra pooja?
explanation: Shri Krishna took up the task of washing the feet of the guests and placing their footwear in a proper place. His knowledge and bravery was incomparable, his character and other attributes were supreme. Hence Bhisma felt that he was the right person for Agra Pooja.

hook: Who created the Mayasabha( the palace of illusions) at Indraprastha?
explanation: Mayasura was the architect of the asuras. He built a palace of illusions(maya sabha) at Indraprastha. This exquisite place had illusions created using multiple mirrors, false rivers etc.

hook: Whom did Krishna kill during Rajasuya Yagna ?
explanation: Shishupala was Krishna's cousin. He hated Krishna and abused him a hundred times during the Rajasuya Yagna. It was then Krishna Killed him with his Sudarshana Chakra.

hook: Which king attacked Dwarka when Krishna was at Indraprasta?
explanation: When Krishna was staying at Indraprastha, Dwarka was attacked by King Salva who used aerial warfare. Hence Kishna had to leave immediately.

hook: What did Krishna advise Yudhisthira?
explanation: Krishna was aware that the Kauravas were jealous of Pandavas because of their prosperity and Rajasuya Yagna. He knew they would harm the Pandavas by inviting them to a game of dice. Hence he warned Yudhisthira not to accept the invitation.

hook: Who advised Duryodhana to invite the Pandavas to a game of dice?
explanation: Kauravas wanted revenge for the insult at Mayasabha. They were also jealous about the wealth and prosperity of Pandavas. Shakuni suggested inviting the Pandavas for a game of dice and he would play on Duryodhana's behalf and win all of the kingdom of Pandavas. He knew of Yudhisthira's weakness for Dice and wanted to make use of it.

hook: Who advises Dhritaraashtra to return the kingdom to Pandavas?
explanation: Dhrstaduymna comes and takes all UpaPandavas. Subhadra’s son, Abhimanyu and Subhadra are taken away by their brother. Five Pandavas and Draupadi go to the forest, Kamayaka-vana. Vidura advises Dhritaraashtra that what he has done is wrong. He tells Dhritaraashtra that Pandavas have been cheated by Shakuni and Duryodhana. He advises Dhritaraashtra to return whatever has been grabbed illegally from Pandavas. He even goes to the extent of saying if Duryodhana does not agree to rule the kingdom jointly, Dhritaraashtra should make Yudishtira the king of Hastinapura. Dhritaraashtra says you are constantly supporting Pandu’s sons even though he is dead and you are my brother. An angry Dhritaraashtra tells Vidura that he can go wherever he wants. Vidura tells Dhritaraashtra that Pandavas will eventually will come back. He says all your children will be killed and the earth will drink their blood.

hook: Who is the only person from Kaurava’s side to come to the forest to console Pandavas?
explanation: Vidura searches for Pandavas in the forest. When Yudishtira sees Vidura coming, he gets confused. He thinks Duryodhana has sent him for a third round of dice to win over the weapons. Even in that state in the forest, he feels he cannot refuse a third invitation! Vidura consoles the Pandavas and advises that the patient one survives. Yudishtira agrees to follow the advice.

hook: Who goes to the forest to get Vidura back?
explanation: After Vidura leaves to the forest, Dhritaraashtra laments to Sanjaya that he has also lost his second brother. Immediately Sanjaya rushes to the forest and meets Vidura and gets him back. The two brothers get united again. This makes Duryodhana a bit nervous as he fears that his father may listen to Vidura and invite the Pandavas back and return the kingdom.

hook: Yudishtira sent Arjuna to obtain weapons. Who did he ask Arjuna to approach for weapons?
explanation: Yudishtira tells Pandavas that it is important to learn and obtain weapons. He asks Arjuna to approach his father Indra and pray at the Gandhamadana mountain. Arjuna does intense Tapas there. He meets a Brahmna. The Brahmna asks him how did you come here as no one is supposed to reach beyond the mountains. The Brahmana reveals his form, which is Indra. He then tells Arjuna to pray to Shiva before he can get the arms.

hook: Mukasura tries to attack Arjuna. In what form does he appear before Arjuna?
explanation: Mukasura tries to attack Arjuna in the form of a wild boar. At the same time, Shiva, along with Parvati and his bhutas come in the form of hunters to bless Arjuna. They hunt the same boar. Arjuna shoots at the boar and so does Shiva. Arjuna tells the hunter that it is his place of Tapas and the hunter should not be there. Arjuna hits the hunter on his head with his sword. The sword breaks on hitting the hunter’s head. The hunter is not hurt. After the hunter overwhelms Arjuna he reveals his forms as Shiva and gives him the Pasupatastra. Although not in the original there is another story. Arjuna had actually got a Shivalinga consecrated there. He was doing Puja to the Shivalinga but the flowers he was offering were going to the hunter instead. He then realizes that the hunter is Shiva. Arjuna goes to Indraloka and returns blessed with weapons from Varuna, Yama and Indra.

hook: How did the Pandavas spend their 12 years in exile?
explanation: Different sages visit Pandavas while they are in forest. Yudishtira seeks advice from all the sages. The Pandavas also visit several tirthas and the sages they meet tell them several stories. Some of the stories narrated to them are Nala Dhamayanti, Savitri and Satyavan, Dharma Vyadha etc. These stories instruct Pandavas about how they should conduct themselves in adversity. From the story of Ramayana, they are told that Rama did not hold any grudge against the world even though he was denied of all the opportunities for no fault of his. He did not lose the stability of his mind. Therefore Pandavas should not lose wisdom (Viveka) in any circumstance.

hook: Gosha Yatra means visiting the cow herd village. What does it also suggest?
explanation: After Pandavas have left for the forest, Duryodhana starts feeling bored. Till then his mind was obsessed with how to hurt Pandavas. Karna tells Duryodhana that he knows where Pandavas are living. Let us go and visit them and show off our wealth to them. He says let us call it as the Gosha Yatra, which means cowherd village yatra. It also suggests that they will do a show-off. Duryodhana says he will ask his father for permission. Karna prevents him from telling his father. He says tell your father that we are just going for a count of the cows. With this excuse Kauravas go on the Gosha Yatra.

hook: Who takes Kauravas as captive near the cowherd villages?
explanation: Kauravas go to the cow herd village and get drunk. They demand alcohol from the villagers and beat them up. They camp around a lake and watch Gandharva women who were taking a bath there. The Gandharva men beat them up. Some of the Kauravas are caught by the Gandharvas.

hook: Which of the Kauravas ran away from the gandharvas?
explanation: Karna gets scared and escapes by getting into Vikarna’s chariot.

hook: What does Yudishtira want to do when he learns of the Gandharvas capturing Kauravas?
explanation: Yudishtira is nearby and wants to help the Kauravas. Arjuna and Bheema get upset and tell Yudishtira that what the Gandharvas did is what we should have done when Draupadi was being shamed._x000D_ _x000D_ यदस्माभिर्नुष्ठेयं गन्धर्वैस्तदनुष्ठितम् ॥महाभारतम्-३-२३१-१५॥_x000D_ _x000D_ Yudishtira says that he is the oldest and the society should respect us brothers as one even if we have divisions within us. He says please go and protect the Kauravas._x000D_ _x000D_ The Pandava brothers rescue Kauravas who feel humiliated that they were rescued by Pandavas. They have lost their face again. When they return, Karna mistakenly thinks that Kauravas had beaten the Gandharvas and congratulates them. This shames Kauravas even more.

hook: The fragrance of a flower overwhelmed Draupadi. What is the name of the flower?
explanation: In Kamyaka-vana Draupadi gets a nice fragrance from a nearby place. It was coming from a flower called Sougandhika. Bheema immediately says he will go and get the pushpa. He follows the fragrance. He encounters a huge, but frail monkey which was actually Anjaneya. Its tail was spread on the path. In our tradition we should not cross over another person. So Bheema tells the monkey why don’t you move the tail a bit. Anjaneya refuses to and says that I am tired and why don’t you move it on your own. Bheema thinks lightly of the monkey and tries. He is unable to do it. The tail of the monkey will not move. Bheema says I am the son of Vayu and Hanuman’s brother. Anjaneya pretends ignorance. Eventually Anjaneya reveals his true form and also shows Vishwarupa. He promises to help Bheema whenever needed.

hook: Who appears in the form of an invisible voice that ends up killing Arjuna, Bheema, Nakula and Sahadeva?
explanation: One day, Pandavas grow very tired and thirsty. Yudishtira asks Nakula to go to a lake and fetch water. He hears a voice that says, “unless you answer questions asked you cannot touch the water.” Nakula does not pay heed to the voice and drinks water. He falls dead right in front of the lake. Next Yudishtira sends Sahadeva who sees his dead brother. He hears the same voice and ignores it and dies too. Arjuna too goes and same voice warns hi. Arjuna starts shooting arrows at the direction of the voice. He also dies. Bheema also meets the same fate. Finally Yudishtira also goes. When Yudisthira hears the voice, he controls himself and starts answering the questions. The voice becomes visible and shows up as a Yaksha. All the brothers are revived from their death and the Yaksa reveals himself as Yudishtira’s father, Dharma.

hook: Who is the unknown voice which asks questions to Pandavas?
explanation: Yakshas are divine beings with special powers.

hook: What is mightier than the earth?
explanation: Mother fulfils all the desires and gives birth and hence she is mightier than earth.

hook: What is faster than the wind?
explanation: The thoughts and the imagination of the mind is varied. It shifts from one idea to another very quickly.

hook: What is taller than the sky?
explanation: Father is compared to the sky and hence you are bound between the sky and earth.

hook: What is the most numerous in humans?
explanation: Human beings have many worries irrespective of their age. Hence Yudhisthira says worries are the most numerous thoughts in the human mind.

hook: What is the greatest wealth?
explanation: To maintain health and prosperity and to use knowledge in the right way wisdom is essential. Hence it is the greatest wealth.

hook: What is the best among all the comforts?
explanation: When we are content then you will be happy and comfortable.

hook: Which of his brothers does Yudhistira choose to come back to life?
explanation: Yudhisthira considered Madri as his mother also along with Kunti. He wanted one son of Madri also to be alive hence he chose Nakula.

hook: What is the second boon Yudhisthira asks?
explanation: Pandavas had to spend one year without being recognised hence he asked for the above boon.

hook: Which kingdom do they go to during Agyatvasa?
explanation: They went to the Matysa kingdom as the king Viratha who ruled there, was very Dharmic.

hook: What role does Yudhisthira take up in agyatavasa?
explanation: He took up this role and taught king Virata the board games.
